name: NextMeeting
author: tylerbarnett3
description: Shows your next Google Meet event in the Omarchy bar and opens it on click.

barWidget.description: Shows your next calendar event and opens Google Meet links.

schema.label: Auto-fetch interval (seconds)
schema.description: How often NextMeeting checks gcalcli for calendar changes. Right-click the widget to refresh manually.

schema.label: Schedule clear text
schema.description: Text shown when there are no more meetings today.

schema.label: Lookahead window (days)
schema.description: How many days ahead NextMeeting should look for the next event.

schema.label: Maximum display characters
schema.description: Maximum length of meeting text before it is truncated with ellipsis.

schema.label: Meet open mode
schema.description: How NextMeeting opens Google Meet links. Chrome app mode is the default for better Google Meet background effects on many Linux systems.

schema.label: Custom open command
schema.description: Used only when Meet open mode is custom-command. The Meet URL is available as $NEXT_MEETING_URL.

schema.label: Chrome app flags
schema.description: Space-separated flags passed to google-chrome-stable in chrome-app mode. Defaults work around common Wayland/GPU quirks on Hyprland; clear this if Chrome runs well natively for you.

schema.label: Calendars
schema.description: Calendars to consider when looking for the next meeting. Leave empty to use every calendar on the account.
schema.emptyText: No calendars found. Run 'gcalcli list' in a terminal.
